critic_raw:
Issue 1: Transaction feed mismatch — baseline from settlement batch, challenger from authorization stream.
Issue 2: Label maturation mismatch — baseline includes pending disputes, challenger uses 60-day settled-only.
Issue 3: Q4 threshold tuned on Q4 and reported on Q4 — threshold self-selection.
Issue 4: Model and pipeline changed simultaneously — gains not attributable to model alone.
defender_raw: Issues 3 and 4: concede. Q4 threshold self-selection and bundled model-pipeline change are valid concerns. Issues 1 and 2: The authorization stream is the production environment; the 60-day maturation rule improves label quality. However, comparing to historical settlement-feed baseline numbers is not apples-to-apples. Both systems must be rescored on a common feed and label definition.
adjudication_raw:
Round 1: Critic raises 4 issues. Defender concedes Issues 3 and 4, partially rebuts Issues 1 and 2 (production realism, label quality). Issues 1 and 2 are not fully rebutted — the comparison uses different populations and label definitions regardless of whether each choice was defensible.

Round 2: Defender acknowledges that both systems must be rescored under common conditions. Issues 1 and 2 resolved as empirical_test_required.

Final verdict: critique_wins. A fair comparison on common conditions may still favor the challenger, but the current packet cannot establish it.
